name: Navas de San Juan – return via Embalsa de Giribaile and Arquillos
name_original: Navas de San Juan – vuelta por Embalsa de Giribaile y Arquillos
region_intro: Road cycling around Navas De San Juan offers diverse terrain on the southern slopes of Sierra Morena, characterized by rolling hills and more demanding ascents. The region features panoramic roads with minimal traffic, traversing Mediterranean pasturelands, cork oak forests, and reservoirs. Cyclists can also experience the vast "sea of olives" and historic towns that define the wider Jaén province. Natural features like the Cerro La Atalaya hill and the Llano de la Estrella plain contribute to the scenic appeal of the area. Best road cycling routes around Navas De San Juan The most popular road cycling route is Úbeda and El Condado Loop, a 62.0 miles (99.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the historic areas of Úbeda and El Condado. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Navas de San Juan – return via Embalsa de Giribaile and Arquillos, a moderate 21.3 miles (34.3 km) path. This route offers views of the Embalsa de Giribaile reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Plaza del Pópulo, Baeza – Plaza de Toros de Úbeda loop from Sabiote, a 28.5 miles (45.8 km) trail leading through the historic towns of Baeza and Úbeda, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Navas De San Juan is defined by rolling hills, olive groves, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult ascents. The routes in Navas De San Juan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 170 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Navas De San Juan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Navas De San Juan?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes documented around Navas De San Juan, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Navas De San Juan?
A: The terrain around Navas De San Juan, situated on the southern slopes of Sierra Morena, features a mix of rolling hills and more demanding ascents. You'll cycle through Mediterranean pasturelands, cork oak forests, and past reservoirs, with the wider Jaén province characterized by its vast "sea of olives" and historic towns.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While the area is known for its varied terrain, including challenging ascents, there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in our current selection. However, 6 of the 15 routes are rated as 'moderate', such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009314861/navas-de-san-juan-return-via-embalsa-de-giribaile-and-arquillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navas de San Juan – return via Embalsa de Giribaile and Arquillos&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the Embalsa de Giribaile reservoir.

Q: Which routes offer a more challenging road cycling experience?
A: For a challenging ride, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009332387/ubeda-and-el-condado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda and El Condado Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 62-mile (99.7 km) trail exploring historic areas. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20127410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hotel Palacio de Úbeda. ***** – Navas de San Juan loop from Navas de San Juan&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 61 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31440004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza del Pópulo, Baeza – Plaza de Toros de Úbeda loop from Sabiote&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 28.5-mile (45.8 km) circular route that takes you through the historic towns of Baeza and Úbeda.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: You can encounter several natural highlights. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009314861/navas-de-san-juan-return-via-embalsa-de-giribaile-and-arquillos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navas de San Juan – return via Embalsa de Giribaile and Arquillos&lt;/a&gt; route offers views of the Embalsa de Giribaile reservoir. Additionally, nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any historic sites or castles to explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. Routes often pass through or near historic towns like Úbeda and Baeza. You can also find several castles nearby, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Sabiote&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilches Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giribaile Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which make for interesting stops.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Navas De San Juan?
A: Navas de San Juan, located in southern Spain, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during these months.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009332387/ubeda-and-el-condado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda and El Condado Loop&lt;/a&gt; cover nearly 100 km. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Toros de Úbeda – Jimena loop from Santa Eulalia&lt;/a&gt;, which extends to almost 68 km, providing ample distance for endurance rides.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Navas De San Juan?
A: The road cycling routes in Navas De San Juan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the panoramic roads with minimal traffic, the diverse scenery ranging from olive groves to cork oak forests, and the blend of challenging climbs with scenic views.

Q: Is it possible to find cafes or accommodation suitable for cyclists in the area?
A: Navas de San Juan and the surrounding historic towns like Úbeda and Baeza are well-equipped to welcome visitors. You'll find various cafes and restaurants for refreshment stops, and a range of accommodation options that cater to tourists, including those on cycling trips.
tips: Small town dating from the middle of the II millennium BC. C. The Church of San Juan Bautista is located in the center of town and is dedicated to the patron saint of the town. The production of olive oil and the running of the bulls play a central role in the life and festivals of the town.

The A-301 highway crosses the Guadalquivir river at its mouth in the Giribaile reservoir. During the rainy season, the river and reservoir teem with life, but can be dry in summer. From the highway you can see the Ariza Bridge, a 15th century masonry bridge over the old road that connected Úbeda with Arquillos. The slopes are dotted with old watchtowers.

waypoints.name: View of the Guadalquivir River and Giribaile Reservoir
